# Project Charter

<!-- Generated by `spec-kitty charter generate` -->

Generated: 2026-05-05T04:44:09Z

## Testing Standards

TDD required for all core modules (DAPyBindQt, DAGui, DAFigure, DAData, DAPyWorkFlow).
Core modules must follow test-first development — write test before implementation.
70%+ coverage required for core modules; non-core modules have no hard coverage requirement.
0 PR approvals required before merge. Test framework: Qt Test. Test files in src/tst/.
On Windows, Qt Test stdout invisible — must use `-o` flag to output to file.

> **Doctrine gap**: Shipped keyword patterns only recognize Python-specific tooling
> (Python test runner, Python type checker). This C++17/Qt project uses Qt Test and MSVC.
> Actual values: framework=Qt Test, type_checking=msvc. Captured in charter.md prose
> but not in extracted governance.yaml.

## Quality Gates

0 approvals required before merge. No pre-commit enforcement. CI via GitHub Actions (build.yml).
Code quality through strict DA-conventions: DA-prefix naming, PIMPL pattern, Q_PROPERTY
property exposure, Q_SIGNALS/Q_SLOTS macros (forbidden: slot/signal lowercase),
Doxygen Chinese comments in .cpp (headers: single-line Chinese comments only).

> **Doctrine gap**: Shipped patterns only recognize Python linting tools.
> This project uses DA-conventions as linting. Actual values: linting=DA-conventions,
> pr_approvals=0, pre_commit_hooks=false. Captured in charter.md prose
> but not in extracted governance.yaml.

## Commit Convention

Conventional commits format. Commit messages should include: task type (implement/fix/docs),
brief description, relevant file list, plan reference if applicable.

## Performance Benchmarks

No hard performance targets. Desktop data analysis application; focus areas are 100K+ row
data processing and chart rendering, but no quantitative goals set yet.

## Branch Strategy

| Branch | Role |
|--------|------|
| master | Stable release branch |
| dev | Integration branch; feature/* merges here first |
| feature/* | Development branches; merge into dev before master |
| workflow-rebuild | Current active branch (rebuild phase) |

1. feature/* branches must merge into dev before entering master
2. dev must be stable before merging into master
3. hotfixes may go directly to master with post-merge dev sync

Deployment: Desktop application installer (Windows primary).
Build: CMake + Visual Studio generator, scripts/build.ps1 automation.
Third-party libs: git submodule, compile and install to bin directory first.

## Governance Activation

```yaml
mission: software-dev
selected_paradigms: [test-first]
selected_directives: [TEST_FIRST]
available_tools: [spec-kitty, git]
template_set: software-dev-default
```

> **Doctrine gap note**: Shipped doctrine contains `test-first` paradigm and `TEST_FIRST`
> directive, matching our TDD requirement. The following governance areas have no shipped
> doctrine match and are encoded as project-specific charter policy:
> - Qt/C++ code conventions (DA prefix, PIMPL, Q_PROPERTY, Q_SIGNALS/Q_SLOTS)
> - Documentation sync policy (architecture changes must update docs, plugin docs must sync)
> - Risk boundaries (src/3rdparty is inviolable)
> - GitFlow branching (master → dev → feature/*)
> - Doxygen Chinese comment conventions

## Policy Summary

- Intent: AI Agent-driven data analysis platform — C++17/Qt directed-graph workflow engine + embedded Python (pandas/numpy/crewAI/LangChain) + interactive charts. Four core modules: Agent orchestration, Workflow engine, Data processing, Chart visualization. AI Agent as core, workflow module as visual carrier for agent call chains, drag-and-drop workflow编排, embedded Python for AI framework integration.

- Languages/Frameworks: C++17, Qt 5.14+/6 (cross-version compatible), pybind11 (Python↔Qt binding), CMake (build system), Python (pandas/numpy/scipy/crewAI/LangChain/LangGraph), Qt Test (testing), SARibbon, qwt, Qt-Advanced-Docking-System, QtPropertyBrowser, spdlog.

- Testing: TDD required. 70%+ coverage for core modules (DAPyBindQt, DAGui, DAFigure, DAData, DAPyWorkFlow). Framework: Qt Test. src/tst/. Windows: `-o` flag for output.

- Quality: No mandatory PR approvals or pre-commit enforcement. CI: GitHub Actions. Conventions: DA-prefix, PIMPL, Q_PROPERTY, Q_SIGNALS/Q_SLOTS.

- Review: No mandatory code review. GitFlow: master → dev → feature/*.

- Performance: No hard targets. Focus: 100K+ rows, chart rendering.

- Deployment: Desktop installer (Windows). CMake + VS. scripts/build.ps1.

## Project Directives

1. Protect third-party libraries — src/3rdparty/ must never be modified (hard red line). Core module stability (DAPyBindQt, workflow engine, plugin interface) is critical. Forbidden: deprecated DAPyNodeConfigDialog/DAPyNodeWidget (use NodeSetting universal parameter panel). Forbidden: Q_OBJECT and Qt signal/slot in QwtPlotItem subclasses.
2. Synchronize documentation with code — architecture changes must update docs; plugin development docs must sync with code.
3. Enforce Qt code conventions — DA prefix in DA namespace; PIMPL pattern (DA_DECLARE_PRIVATE, DA_D, DA_DC, DA_PIMPL_CONSTRUCT); Q_PROPERTY for property exposure; Q_SIGNALS/Q_SLOTS (never slot/signal lowercase); Doxygen Chinese in .cpp only; Qt5/Qt6 compat via Qt5Qt6Compat_* macros.
4. Test-first for core modules — TDD required for DAPyBindQt, DAGui, DAFigure, DAData, DAPyWorkFlow. Write tests before implementation.

## Amendment Process

Simple discussion followed by direct charter edit. No formal approval process.
When revising, update charter.md and run `spec-kitty charter sync` to re-extract structured config.

## Exception Policy

Emergency fixes may temporarily bypass governance rules (fix bug first, add tests later),
but must remediate all governance requirements afterward. Experimental development also
allows temporary bypass, but must complete before merging into dev branch.
Exceptions noted in commit messages.
